summary: Solid structure but has type safety holes (unsafe casts in implementation), missing edge case tests (network errors, non-JSON responses, fallback error message path), and test assertions use non-null assertions and as-casts.

suggestions.description: Unsafe type assertion casts unknown response to domain types
suggestions.rationale: getBoard and getFeed cast the unvalidated response body to BoardState and FeedEvent[] using as-casts. If the server returns malformed data, the client will silently pass it through, violating the type contract. The CLAUDE.md guidelines explicitly prohibit any types and type assertions.
suggestions.suggestion: Add runtime validation (e.g., a type guard or schema validator) before returning, or at minimum type the request function generically with a validator callback.

suggestions.description: as Record<string,unknown> cast in error extraction
suggestions.rationale: After checking body is an object with an error property via the in operator, the code still uses an as-cast to access the property. TypeScript narrowing after the in check should suffice, or use a type guard.
suggestions.suggestion: Use a narrowing type guard function: function hasError(b: unknown): b is { error: unknown } { return typeof b === "object" && b \!== null && "error" in b; } then call String(b.error) without casting.

suggestions.description: No test for the fallback error message path
suggestions.rationale: The implementation has two branches in error handling: one when the body has an error field, one that falls back to Request failed. Only the error-field branch is tested. The fallback branch is dead code from the tests perspective.
suggestions.suggestion: Add a test where the error response body lacks an error field (e.g., { message: "oops" }) and assert the thrown error contains "Request failed".

suggestions.description: No test for network-level failures (fetch rejection)
suggestions.rationale: If fetch itself throws (network down, DNS failure, etc.), the promise rejection propagates unhandled. Tests should verify this behavior to document whether the client wraps or passes through network errors.
suggestions.suggestion: Add a test where fetchSpy.mockRejectedValue(new TypeError("fetch failed")) and verify the error propagates or is wrapped.

suggestions.description: No test for non-JSON error responses
suggestions.rationale: If the server returns a non-JSON body (e.g., HTML error page), response.json() will throw. The implementation has no handling for this case. A test should document the expected behavior.
suggestions.suggestion: Add a test where fetch returns a Response with a non-JSON body and a non-OK status, and verify the error behavior.

suggestions.description: Auth header test only checks one method despite claiming every request
suggestions.rationale: The test is named sends Bearer token on every request but only calls getBoard. If auth injection were broken for POST-based methods specifically, this test would not catch it.
suggestions.suggestion: Either rename to sends Bearer token on GET request or add a second assertion using a POST method (e.g., createEpic) to actually prove the claim.

suggestions.description: Non-null assertions and as-casts throughout test assertions
suggestions.rationale: Every test uses fetchSpy.mock.calls[0]\! as [string, RequestInit] which is a non-null assertion plus a type cast. The CLAUDE.md guidelines prohibit type assertions. These hide potential issues if the spy was not called.
suggestions.suggestion: Assert fetchSpy.mock.calls.length before accessing elements, and use a typed helper function that validates the shape rather than casting.

suggestions.description: Content-Type: application/json sent on GET requests
suggestions.rationale: GET requests (getBoard, getFeed) do not have bodies, so Content-Type is meaningless on them. Some servers or proxies may react unexpectedly to Content-Type on bodyless requests.
suggestions.suggestion: Only set Content-Type header when options.method is POST or PATCH, or when a body is present.
